items.headline: Planning Commission to hear overview of SB 79 affordable housing near transit law
items.summary: The Heritage Preservation Commission will receive a presentation on California's Abundant and Affordable Homes Near Transit Act (SB 79, passed in 2025). The law affects what types of housing development can occur near transit in California cities, including Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Six-unit apartment building permitted on North 6th Street
items.summary: A new 6-unit apartment building received a permit at 445 N 6th St in what appears to be a relatively dense urban infill area. This adds housing units in a walkable neighborhood location.

items.headline: Santa Clara Tourism Improvement District renewed; Discover Santa Clara to continue management
items.summary: The city council voted to renew the Santa Clara Tourism Improvement District and approved an agreement with Discover Santa Clara to administer it. The district collects fees from hotels and other tourism businesses to fund destination marketing and visitor services.

items.headline: Four parcels in Santa Clara to be merged into single property
items.summary: The city council approved a Vesting Tentative Parcel Map to consolidate four existing parcels into one. The merger could facilitate future development or redevelopment of the combined property.

items.headline: Council to adopt park dedication ordinance for Measure E site
items.summary: Palo Alto City Council is set to adopt an ordinance dedicating a portion of the Measure E site as parkland. The item is exempt from full environmental review under state CEQA regulations.

items.headline: Council to review Baylands Golf Links driving range expansion feasibility
items.summary: City Council will review a financial feasibility study update for an expansion of the driving range at Baylands Golf Links on June 23.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study Housing Element update
items.summary: The Planning Commission scheduled a study session on a Housing Element update. The agenda entry cuts off before detailing what changes are being considered, so specifics on units, zoning, or timeline are not available in this document.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study artificial turf policy for Sunnyvale
items.summary: A special Planning Commission study session on June 22 will examine an artificial turf policy. No details on scope, restrictions, or affected properties were provided in the agenda.
